discrepancies.subject: Q-198-4 A7 cover-letter audit timing — verdict diverges from dispatch framing
discrepancies.dispatch_implicit_prior: Q-198-1 A7 lists 'Cover-letter-differentiation audit for Items 16/17/20 (post-ExpMath rejection re-routings; was RULE-1-tabled; now actionable)' with phrasing suggesting reactive-on-rejection-signal
discrepancies.verdict_recommendation: Fire now, not on rejection signal; audit value is in *protecting against* next rejection so reactive forfeits benefit; operator bandwidth small (~1 session); ExpMath re-routing pattern structurally informative regardless of individual item outcomes
discrepancies.resolution_recommended: Adopt Q-198-4 amendment: schedule A7 firing 2026-05-14 per AGGREGATE_72H Day-4 slot; surface to operator at next session

discrepancies.subject: PROMPT 197 SUPERSEDED naming convention — confirm before rename
discrepancies.verdict_risk_flag: RISK_FLAGS bullet 5: 'Confirm the project's existing supersession convention before renaming; some workflows use _SUPERSEDED suffix, others use a superseded/ subdirectory. Mismatch could break grep-based prompt-index tooling.'
discrepancies.agent_action: Renamed 197_*.txt -> 197_*_SUPERSEDED.txt at 2026-05-11 ~21:35 JST (BEFORE verdict received; same _STATUS_TAG.txt suffix pattern as 191_*_HALTED.txt + 194/195/196_*_EXECUTED.txt)
discrepancies.verification: Convention matches existing in-tree pattern (_HALTED, _EXECUTED). 4 prior precedents in same directory; no superseded/ subdirectory pattern observed. Rename is consistent.
